figure.title: Master Key Rollover Sequence Diagram
figure.purpose: Illustrates the exact message exchange sequence between Accelerator A (TX) and Accelerator B (RX) during a Master Key Rollover.

technical_summary: When Accelerator A's master key expires, it derives a new master key (MKA2) and new derived keys.
Accelerator A sends a 'KeyRollMSG.ReqChannel' request encrypted with the old key.
Accelerator A immediately switches to the new key for subsequent requests.
Accelerator B acknowledges on the write response stream.
Accelerator B then initiates handshakes for the read response and write response streams using 'KeyRollMSG.RdRspChannel' and 'KeyRollMSG.WrRspChannel'.
Both sides switch to the new derived keys in lockstep immediately following the respective handshake messages.

ordering: ReqChannel request -> TX switch -> ReqChannel response -> RX switch
RdRspChannel request -> RdRspChannel response -> TX/RX switch
WrRspChannel request -> WrRspChannel response -> TX/RX switch

critical_facts: The sequence diagram confirms that the key switch happens on the 'very next' message after the handshake, guaranteeing no messages are sent with ambiguous encryption states.
